name: Network Diagnostics Toolkit
description: Diagnose network connectivity, DNS resolution, port availability, and routing issues using standard Linux networking tools.

steps.title: Check network interfaces and IP addresses
steps.description: View all network interfaces and their assigned IP addresses.

steps.title: Test DNS resolution
steps.description: Verify that DNS is resolving domain names correctly.
steps.tip: Use 'dig @8.8.8.8 domain.com' to test against a specific DNS server.

steps.title: Check if a remote port is reachable
steps.description: Test TCP connectivity to a specific host and port.
steps.tip: The -w flag sets a timeout. Use -u for UDP ports.

steps.title: Trace the network path to a destination
steps.description: See each network hop between you and the destination to identify where packets are being dropped.

steps.title: Check listening ports
steps.description: View all ports that are currently listening for connections.
steps.tip: -t for TCP, -l for listening, -n for numeric ports, -p for process names.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Network Diagnostics Toolkit
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Network Interfaces ==="
ip -brief addr

echo ""
echo "=== Default Route ==="
ip route | grep default

echo ""
echo "=== DNS Configuration ==="
cat /etc/resolv.conf | grep -v '^#'

echo ""
echo "=== DNS Resolution Test ==="
dig google.com +short 2>/dev/null || nslookup google.com 2>/dev/null | grep Address

echo ""
echo "=== Listening Ports ==="
ss -tlnp | head -20

echo ""
echo "=== Active Connections ==="
ss -tn state established | head -10

echo ""
echo "=== Diagnostic Commands ==="
echo "Ping:         ping -c 4 <host>"
echo "DNS:          dig <domain> +short"
echo "Port check:   nc -zv <host> <port>"
echo "Traceroute:   traceroute -n <host>"
echo "Listening:    ss -tlnp"
echo "Connections:  ss -tn state established"

faq.question: What is the difference between ss and netstat?
faq.answer: ss is the modern replacement for netstat. It is faster, provides more information, and is included in all modern Linux distributions. netstat is deprecated.

faq.question: How do I check if a firewall is blocking traffic?
faq.answer: Use 'sudo iptables -L -n' or 'sudo nft list ruleset' to view firewall rules. Test connectivity with nc or telnet to confirm whether traffic is reaching the host.
